title: Backend Infrastructure - Database Models, API Endpoints, and LLM Integration
description: Create database models for AI analysis results, API endpoints for AI analysis, and LLM integration service. This is the foundational layer that all other components depend on.

files.change: Add endpoints: GET /api/ai/analysis/{symbol}, POST /api/ai/batch-analysis, GET /api/ai/analysis/status/{task_id}, GET /api/ai/market-sentiment, GET /api/ai/company-events/{symbol}
Add AIAnalysisResult model: id, symbol, recommendation, confidence, sentiment_score, technical_score, event_signals, news_impact, risk_level, analysis_date, created_at
Create OpenAI-compatible LLM service for investment insight generation. Include: base_url config, api_key env var, model selection, prompt templates for investment analysis
Create request/response schemas: AIAnalysisRequest, AIAnalysisResponse, BatchAnalysisRequest, MarketSentimentResponse, CompanyEventsResponse

implementation: Create AIAnalysisResult SQLAlchemy model in db/mysql.py with fields: symbol, recommendation (买入/持有/卖出/观望), confidence (0-1), sentiment_score (-1 to 1), technical_score (0-100), event_signals (JSON), news_impact (-1 to 1), risk_level (高/中/低), analysis_date, created_at
Create Pydantic schemas in schemas/ai_analysis.py for request/response validation
Implement LLM service in services/llm_service.py with OpenAI-compatible interface: async def generate_insight(symbol, analysis_data) -> str
Add API endpoints in api/main.py: GET /api/ai/analysis/{symbol}, POST /api/ai/batch-analysis, GET /api/ai/analysis/status/{task_id}, GET /api/ai/market-sentiment, GET /api/ai/company-events/{symbol}
Add database migration for AIAnalysisResult table
Write unit tests for LLM service with mocked API responses

reference.pattern: follow existing FastAPI endpoint pattern in api/main.py and SQLAlchemy model pattern in db/mysql.py
reference.examples: Use watchlist_service.py as reference for service layer pattern, mysql.py for model definition

criteria: AIAnalysisResult model has 11 fields as specified
5 API endpoints implemented with Pydantic validation
LLM service supports OpenAI-compatible API with configurable endpoint
All endpoints respond within 200ms p95 (use async/await)

rationale.chosen_approach: Build foundational infrastructure first to enable parallel development of dependent layers
alternatives_considered: Start with data collection - rejected because analysis engine needs DB schema to persist results
Start with frontend - rejected because no API to integrate with
decision_factors: Enables parallel development after infrastructure is ready
Follows strict layer separation (Controller → Service → Model → DB)
rationale.tradeoffs: Time to market delayed until infrastructure is complete, but subsequent tasks can proceed faster

manual_checks: Verify LLM service connects to custom endpoint
success_metrics: All unit tests pass with 100% success rate

risks.description: LLM API latency could exceed 200ms requirement
risks.mitigation: Use async endpoints, cache LLM responses for repeated queries, implement timeout with fallback
risks.fallback: Return cached analysis with staleness indicator

risks.description: Database schema changes require migration coordination
risks.mitigation: Use Alembic for migrations, maintain backward compatibility
risks.fallback: Add new columns with defaults rather than altering existing

interfaces: LLMService: async generate_insight(symbol: str, analysis_data: dict) -> str - generates AI investment insight
AIAnalysisRepository: save(result: AIAnalysisResult), get_by_symbol(symbol: str), get_by_task_id(task_id: str) - data access
functions: async def get_ai_analysis(symbol: str) -> AIAnalysisResponse - main analysis endpoint
async def batch_analyze(symbols: list[str]) -> BatchAnalysisResponse - batch analysis with task_id for polling
async def get_analysis_status(task_id: str) -> AnalysisStatusResponse - poll batch analysis status

classes.name: AIAnalysisResult
classes.purpose: Database model for persisting AI analysis results

classes.name: LLMService
classes.purpose: OpenAI-compatible LLM integration for investment insights
